name: Japanese N4 Grammar
description: Master 133 intermediate JLPT N4 grammar patterns — from conditionals and te-form auxiliaries to causative-passive, honorific speech, and appearance/hearsay expressions. Fill in the blank to prove your grammar intuition.

facts.quizQuestion:
もっと早く起き{___}、電車に間に合ったのに。
(If I had woken up earlier, I would have made the train.)
facts.explanation: れば (conditional if/then) — もっと早く起きれば、電車に間に合ったのに。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
お金があ{___}、新しいパソコンを買うのに。
(If I had money, I would buy a new computer.)
facts.explanation: れば (conditional if/then) — お金があれば、新しいパソコンを買うのに。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
天気がよけ{___}、公園でピクニックをしましょう。
(If the weather is good, let's have a picnic in the park.)
facts.explanation: れば (conditional if/then) — 天気がよければ、公園でピクニックをしましょう。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
勉強す{___}、試験に合格できると思います。
(I think if you study, you can pass the exam.)
facts.explanation: れば (conditional if/then) — 勉強すれば、試験に合格できると思います。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
火事の{___}、すぐに非常口から逃げてください。
(In the event of a fire, please escape immediately through the emergency exit.)
facts.explanation: 場合は (in the event of; in case) — 火事の場合は、すぐに非常口から逃げてください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
遅刻する{___}、必ず連絡してください。
(If you are going to be late, please be sure to contact us.)
facts.explanation: 場合は (in the event of; in case) — 遅刻する場合は、必ず連絡してください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
緊急の{___}、この番号に電話してください。
(In case of emergency, please call this number.)
facts.explanation: 場合は (in the event of; in case) — 緊急の場合は、この番号に電話してください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
その夜半に目覚め{___}呼吸が酷く不規則で苦しくなっていました。
(When I woke up late that night my breathing had become horribly irregular and difficult.)
facts.explanation: たら (if; after; when (conditional/temporal)) — その夜半に目覚めたら呼吸が酷く不規則で苦しくなっていました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
私ももし、イギリスに行っ{___}そうなるのか。
(If I go to England will it be like that for me as well?)
facts.explanation: たら (if; after; when (conditional/temporal)) — 私ももし、イギリスに行ったらそうなるのか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
まず説明書を読ん{___}ＩＫＥＡの家具のパックが作れる。
(If you read the instructions first then you can make an IKEA flat furniture pack.)
facts.explanation: だら (if; after; when (conditional/temporal)) — まず説明書を読んだらＩＫＥＡの家具のパックが作れる。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
「あなたに何かあげられ{___}なぁ…」
("I wish that I could give you something...")
facts.explanation: たら (if; after; when (conditional/temporal)) — 「あなたに何かあげられたらなぁ…」
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
１００万円獲得し{___}、どうしますか。
(If you won a million yen, what would you do?)
facts.explanation: たら (if; after; when (conditional/temporal)) — １００万円獲得したら、どうしますか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
医者に行っ{___}。
(Why don't you go to the doctor?)
facts.explanation: たらどうですか (why don't you; how about doing) — 医者に行ったらどうですか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
もっと野菜を食べ{___}。
(How about eating more vegetables?)
facts.explanation: たらどうですか (why don't you; how about doing) — もっと野菜を食べたらどうですか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
先生に相談し{___}。
(Why don't you consult with the teacher?)
facts.explanation: たらどうでしょうか (why don't you; how about doing) — 先生に相談したらどうでしょうか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
どうし{___}。
(What should I do?)
facts.explanation: たらいいですか (what should I do?; what would be good?) — どうしたらいいですか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この薬はいつ飲んだ{___}。
(When should I take this medicine?)
facts.explanation: らいいですか (what should I do?; what would be good?) — この薬はいつ飲んだらいいですか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
駅までどう行っ{___}。
(How should I go to the station?)
facts.explanation: たらいいですか (what should I do?; what would be good?) — 駅までどう行ったらいいですか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
日本語を勉強する{___}、毎日練習することが大切です。
(If you're going to study Japanese, it's important to practice every day.)
facts.explanation: なら (if; in the case that; if it is the case that) — 日本語を勉強するなら、毎日練習することが大切です。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
東京に行く{___}、浅草に寄ってみてください。
(If you're going to Tokyo, please stop by Asakusa.)
facts.explanation: なら (if; in the case that; if it is the case that) — 東京に行くなら、浅草に寄ってみてください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
君が行かない{___}、私も行きません。
(If you're not going, I won't go either.)
facts.explanation: なら (if; in the case that; if it is the case that) — 君が行かないなら、私も行きません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
安い{___}、二つ買います。
(If it's cheap, I'll buy two.)
facts.explanation: なら (if; in the case that; if it is the case that) — 安いなら、二つ買います。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
春になる{___}、桜が咲きます。
(When spring comes, the cherry blossoms bloom.)
facts.explanation: と (whenever A happens, B naturally happens; if (natural consequence)) — 春になると、桜が咲きます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
このボタンを押す{___}、ドアが開きます。
(When you press this button, the door opens.)
facts.explanation: と (whenever A happens, B naturally happens; if (natural consequence)) — このボタンを押すと、ドアが開きます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
北に行く{___}、山が見えます。
(If you go north, you can see mountains.)
facts.explanation: と (whenever A happens, B naturally happens; if (natural consequence)) — 北に行くと、山が見えます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
お酒を飲みすぎる{___}、体に悪いです。
(If you drink too much alcohol, it's bad for your body.)
facts.explanation: と (whenever A happens, B naturally happens; if (natural consequence)) — お酒を飲みすぎると、体に悪いです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
友達に本を貸し{___}ました。
(I lent a book to my friend.)
facts.explanation: てあげ (to do something for someone (speaker or in-group acts for out-group)) — 友達に本を貸してあげました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
子供に宿題を手伝っ{___}ました。
(I helped the child with their homework.)
facts.explanation: てあげ (to do something for someone (speaker or in-group acts for out-group)) — 子供に宿題を手伝ってあげました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
道に迷っ{___}人に道を教えてあげました。
(I gave directions to a person who was lost.)
facts.explanation: ている (to do something for someone (speaker or in-group acts for out-group)) — 道に迷っている人に道を教えてあげました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
先生に日本語を教え{___}いました。
(I had the teacher teach me Japanese.)
facts.explanation: てもら (to get/have someone do something for you; to receive the favor of) — 先生に日本語を教えてもらいました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
母に料理を作っ{___}いたいです。
(I want my mother to cook for me.)
facts.explanation: てもら (to get/have someone do something for you; to receive the favor of) — 母に料理を作ってもらいたいです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
田中さんが駅まで送っ{___}ました。
(Tanaka-san gave me a ride to the station.)
facts.explanation: てくれ (to do something for me/us (someone does a favor for the speaker)) — 田中さんが駅まで送ってくれました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
友達が誕生日にケーキを作っ{___}ました。
(My friend made a cake for my birthday.)
facts.explanation: てくれ (to do something for me/us (someone does a favor for the speaker)) — 友達が誕生日にケーキを作ってくれました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
先生が分かりやすく説明し{___}ました。
(The teacher explained it to me in an easy-to-understand way.)
facts.explanation: てくれ (to do something for me/us (someone does a favor for the speaker)) — 先生が分かりやすく説明してくれました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
猫にえさをやっ{___}りました。
(I fed the cat (for it).)
facts.explanation: てや (to do for (casual/condescending; speaker acts for someone of lower status)) — 猫にえさをやってやりました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
弟の自転車を直し{___}。
(I fixed my little brother's bicycle for him.)
facts.explanation: てやった (to do for (casual/condescending; speaker acts for someone of lower status)) — 弟の自転車を直してやった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
新しいレストランで食べ{___}ました。
(I tried eating at the new restaurant.)
facts.explanation: てみ (to try doing; to do and see (experiment with an action)) — 新しいレストランで食べてみました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
その映画を見{___}いです。
(I want to try watching that movie.)
facts.explanation: てみた (to try doing; to do and see (experiment with an action)) — その映画を見てみたいです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
日本語で手紙を書い{___}ました。
(I tried writing a letter in Japanese.)
facts.explanation: てみ (to try doing; to do and see (experiment with an action)) — 日本語で手紙を書いてみました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
旅行の前に、ホテルを予約し{___}ました。
(I reserved a hotel in advance before the trip.)
facts.explanation: ておき (to do something in advance; to do and leave it that way) — 旅行の前に、ホテルを予約しておきました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
明日の会議のために、資料を準備し{___}ください。
(Please prepare the materials in advance for tomorrow's meeting.)
facts.explanation: ておいて (to do something in advance; to do and leave it that way) — 明日の会議のために、資料を準備しておいてください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
窓を開け{___}ほうがいいですよ。
(You should leave the window open.)
facts.explanation: ておいた (to do something in advance; to do and leave it that way) — 窓を開けておいたほうがいいですよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
大切な書類をなくし{___}。
(I accidentally lost the important documents.)
facts.explanation: てしまいました (to finish completely; to do something by accident/regrettably) — 大切な書類をなくしてしまいました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
ケーキを全部食べ{___}。
(I ended up eating all the cake.)
facts.explanation: てしまった (to finish completely; to do something by accident/regrettably) — ケーキを全部食べてしまった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
財布を家に忘れ{___}。
(I accidentally left my wallet at home.)
facts.explanation: ちゃった (to finish completely; to do something by accident/regrettably) — 財布を家に忘れちゃった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
宿題をもうやっ{___}。
(I've already finished my homework.)
facts.explanation: てしまいました (to finish completely; to do something by accident/regrettably) — 宿題をもうやってしまいました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
これからも日本語を勉強し{___}。
(I will continue to study Japanese from now on.)
facts.explanation: ていきます (to continue doing; to go on doing; (action moves away from speaker)) — これからも日本語を勉強していきます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
気温がだんだん下がっ{___}。
(The temperature gradually keeps going down.)
facts.explanation: ていきます (to continue doing; to go on doing; (action moves away from speaker)) — 気温がだんだん下がっていきます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は荷物を持っ{___}きました。
(He took the luggage and left.)
facts.explanation: てい (to continue doing; to go on doing; (action moves away from speaker)) — 彼は荷物を持っていきました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
コーヒーを買っ{___}ください。
(Please go buy some coffee and bring it back.)
facts.explanation: てきて (to come to be; to continue; to start (action moves toward speaker or begins)) — コーヒーを買ってきてください。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
もっと静かにし{___}。
(I want you to be quieter.)
facts.explanation: てほしいです (I want you to do; I want (someone else) to do) — もっと静かにしてほしいです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女に早く来{___}。
(I wanted her to come earlier.)
facts.explanation: てほしかった (I want you to do; I want (someone else) to do) — 彼女に早く来てほしかった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
子供たちに野菜を食べ{___}。
(I want the children to eat vegetables.)
facts.explanation: てほしいです (I want you to do; I want (someone else) to do) — 子供たちに野菜を食べてほしいです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
日本語を勉強し{___}。
(I'm glad I studied Japanese.)
facts.explanation: てよかったです (I'm glad that; it was good that (expression of relief)) — 日本語を勉強してよかったです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
傘を持ってき{___}。雨が降ってきた。
(I'm glad I brought an umbrella. It started raining.)
facts.explanation: てよかった (I'm glad that; it was good that (expression of relief)) — 傘を持ってきてよかった。雨が降ってきた。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
早めに予約し{___}。満席でした。
(I'm glad I made a reservation early. It was fully booked.)
facts.explanation: てよかったです (I'm glad that; it was good that (expression of relief)) — 早めに予約してよかったです。満席でした。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
遅れ{___}。
(I'm sorry for being late.)
facts.explanation: てすみません (I'm sorry for doing; sorry to have done) — 遅れてすみません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
うるさくし{___}。
(I'm sorry for being noisy.)
facts.explanation: てすみませんでした (I'm sorry for doing; sorry to have done) — うるさくしてすみませんでした。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
ご迷惑をかけ{___}。
(I'm sorry for causing you trouble.)
facts.explanation: てすみません (I'm sorry for doing; sorry to have done) — ご迷惑をかけてすみません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
もう一度説明し{___}。
(Could you please explain it one more time?)
facts.explanation: ていただけませんか (could you please do; would you mind doing (polite request)) — もう一度説明していただけませんか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この書類にサインし{___}。
(Would you mind signing this document?)
facts.explanation: ていただけませんか (could you please do; would you mind doing (polite request)) — この書類にサインしていただけませんか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
少し待っ{___}。
(Could you please wait a moment?)
facts.explanation: ていただけませんか (could you please do; would you mind doing (polite request)) — 少し待っていただけませんか。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
朝ご飯を食べ{___}、学校に行きました。
(I ate breakfast and went to school.)
facts.explanation: て (conjunctive; so; because of (て-form as clause connector)) — 朝ご飯を食べて、学校に行きました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は学生で、アルバイトもし{___}います。
(He is a student and also works part-time.)
facts.explanation: て (conjunctive; so; because of (て-form as clause connector)) — 彼は学生で、アルバイトもしています。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
財布をなくし{___}、困りました。
(I lost my wallet and was in trouble.)
facts.explanation: て (conjunctive; so; because of (て-form as clause connector)) — 財布をなくして、困りました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
これは、食べられ{___}ではありません。
(This doesn't look like it's edible.)
facts.explanation: そう (looks like; appears; seems (based on direct visual evidence)) — これは、食べられそうではありません。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
雪が降り{___}ね。
(Looks like it's going to snow, doesn't it?)
facts.explanation: そうです (looks like; appears; seems (based on direct visual evidence)) — 雪が降りそうですね。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
美味し{___}なデザートですね。
(That looks like a delicious dessert!)
facts.explanation: そう (looks like; appears; seems (based on direct visual evidence)) — 美味しそうなデザートですね。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
「起こしてくれればよかったのに」翌朝ロンが不機嫌{___}にいった。
("You could have woken me up..." Ron said the following morning in a grumpy seeming way.)
facts.explanation: そう (looks like; appears; seems (based on direct visual evidence)) — 「起こしてくれればよかったのに」翌朝ロンが不機嫌そうにいった。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
この本は高{___}。
(This book looks expensive.)
facts.explanation: そうです (looks like; appears; seems (based on direct visual evidence)) — この本は高そうです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
明日は雨が降る{___}。
(I heard it will rain tomorrow.)
facts.explanation: そうです (I heard that; it is said that (second-hand information)) — 明日は雨が降るそうです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
田中さんは来週結婚する{___}。
(I heard Tanaka-san is getting married next week.)
facts.explanation: そうです (I heard that; it is said that (second-hand information)) — 田中さんは来週結婚するそうです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
あのレストランは料理がおいしい{___}。
(I heard the food at that restaurant is delicious.)
facts.explanation: そうです (I heard that; it is said that (second-hand information)) — あのレストランは料理がおいしいそうです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は悲し{___}話していました。
(He was talking in a sad-seeming way.)
facts.explanation: そうに (seems like; looks like (adverbial/adjectival form of そうだ)) — 彼は悲しそうに話していました。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
おいし{___}料理が並んでいます。
(Delicious-looking dishes are lined up.)
facts.explanation: そうな (seems like; looks like (adverbial/adjectival form of そうだ)) — おいしそうな料理が並んでいます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
子供たちが楽し{___}遊んでいます。
(The children are playing in a fun-seeming way.)
facts.explanation: そうに (seems like; looks like (adverbial/adjectival form of そうだ)) — 子供たちが楽しそうに遊んでいます。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼はもう日本を떠った{___}。
(It seems he has already left Japan.)
facts.explanation: らしいです (it seems; apparently; I hear that (inference from evidence/rumor)) — 彼はもう日本を떠ったらしいです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
外が騒がしいですね。事故があった{___}。
(It's noisy outside. It seems there was an accident.)
facts.explanation: らしいです (it seems; apparently; I hear that (inference from evidence/rumor)) — 外が騒がしいですね。事故があったらしいです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
今年の夏は暑い{___}よ。
(I hear this summer is going to be hot.)
facts.explanation: らしいです (it seems; apparently; I hear that (inference from evidence/rumor)) — 今年の夏は暑いらしいですよ。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼女は本当に先生{___}先生です。
(She is a truly teacher-like teacher.)
facts.explanation: らしい (it seems; apparently; I hear that (inference from evidence/rumor)) — 彼女は本当に先生らしい先生です。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
彼は風邪をひいた{___}。
(It appears he has caught a cold.)
facts.explanation: ようです (appears; seems; looks as if (inference based on observation)) — 彼は風邪をひいたようです。
facts.visualDescription: Japanese grammar study card with sentence completion

facts.quizQuestion:
外の様子を見ると、雨が降った{___}。
(Looking at the outside, it seems it rained.)
facts.explanation: ようです (appears; seems; looks as if (inference based on observation)) — 外の様子を見ると、雨が降ったようです。

synonymGroups.reason: なければいけない and なければならない are interchangeable in meaning; ならない is slightly more formal/written.
ようだ and みたいだ are largely interchangeable in meaning; みたいだ is casual spoken, ようだ is neutral-to-formal.
にくい and づらい both mean 'difficult to do' and are often interchangeable; づらい carries a more uncomfortable or emotionally painful nuance.
しかし、でも、and けれども all mean "but/however"; しかし is formal written, でも is casual, けれども can connect clauses or start sentences.
かな and かしら are both sentence-final particles for self-directed wondering; かしら is associated with feminine or older-generation speech.
ので and なので both express reason/cause; ので follows plain verb/adjective forms, なので follows noun/na-adjective + な.
ば and たら both express conditional "if"; たら is more colloquial and can express sequential events, ば is more formal and tends toward general conditions.
てあげる and てやる both mean "to do something for someone"; てやる is casual/masculine and can sound condescending, てあげる is the neutral/polite form.
